assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 14: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' — identical to claude-md-configured.md line 16.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 15: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new row with serena_ui as Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 19-23 match claude-md-configured.md lines 19-23 exactly: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has '## Added' section (lines 7-13) listing new trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation, and '## Preserved (unchanged)' section (lines 16-36) listing the existing trustify-backend row, all Jira fields, Code Intelligence content, and Bug Configuration fields — each marked as '(preserved)'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 27: 'serena_backend: already in Registry — **skipping**' and line 28: 'serena_ui: **not in Registry** — needs configuration'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 27-41: naming convention text ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), serena_backend find_symbol example (lines 32-36), and serena_backend limitation (line 40) are all preserved identically. Only addition is line 41: '- `serena_ui`: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 43-47: Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks — all identical to claude-md-configured.md. changes-log.md line 36: 'Status: **Bug Configuration is up to date** — no changes needed'. discovery-log.md line 83: '**Bug Configuration is up to date** — skipping.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 87-94: 'Security Configuration does not exist in CLAUDE.md.' followed by the prompt offered to the user and 'User response: **declined**' and '**Security Configuration was not scaffolded** — skipping'. changes-log.md lines 48-51 confirms: 'User was asked whether to enable security triage and **declined**' / 'Skipped per user preference'. claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section.
